req.title: Diagnostic report (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three testable behaviours: (1) a valid DiagnosticReport with populated Observation references is accepted and stored (positive / EP), (2) a DiagnosticReport with an empty result array is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative / NEG), and (3) a DiagnosticReport with exactly one Observation reference sits on the boundary of the valid partition (boundary / BVA). Each test also asserts that an AuditEvent is emitted, covering the end-to-end audit trail mandated by R-HLT-087. A fourth test exercises the state transition from 'registered' to 'final' status to cover STC, and a fifth uses a decision table to verify that missing vs. present subject reference produces the correct accept/reject decision (DT).

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations
preconditions: At least two Observation resources pre-created on the server via <create_observation_fixture>
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing two valid Observation IDs
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician bearer token
Query /AuditEvent for the resulting report ID
expected: Response body result array length equals 2
At least one AuditEvent references the new DiagnosticReport ID
trace: R-HLT-087: report stored when Observation links are present
R-HLT-087: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests
from conftest import BASE_URL, clinician_headers, create_observation

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations():
    obs1 = create_observation()
    obs2 = create_observation()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Panel"},
        "result": [
            {"reference": f"Observation/{obs1}"},
            {"reference": f"Observation/{obs2}"}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "DiagnosticReport"
    report_id = body["id"]
    assert len(body["result"]) == 2
    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"DiagnosticReport/{report_id}"},
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["total"] >= 1

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with an empty result array
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician bearer token
Query /AuditEvent to confirm rejection is also audited
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or error detail indicating missing references
An AuditEvent with outcome 8 (serious failure) is recorded
trace: R-HLT-087: AuditEvent emitted for every operation including failures
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL, clinician_headers

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Panel"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body
    assert body["resourceType"] in ("OperationOutcome", "error")
    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"outcome": "8", "action": "C"},
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["total"] >= 1

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation
preconditions: Exactly one Observation resource pre-created via <create_observation_fixture>
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing exactly one Observation ID
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician bearer token
expected: Response body result array length equals exactly 1
AuditEvent entry exists for the created report
trace: R-HLT-087: boundary between empty (invalid) and non-empty (valid) result array
R-HLT-087: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL, clinician_headers, create_observation

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation():
    obs_id = create_observation()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Single-obs panel"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body["result"]) == 1
    report_id = body["id"]
    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"DiagnosticReport/{report_id}"},
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["total"] >= 1

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_status_transition_registered_to_final
actions: POST DiagnosticReport with status 'registered' and one Observation reference
PUT updated DiagnosticReport with status changed to 'final'
Verify final state and two AuditEvents (create + update)
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status 'registered'
Subsequent PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'final'
At least two AuditEvents recorded for the report ID
trace: R-HLT-087: state transition registered->final is a valid operation path
R-HLT-087: AuditEvent emitted for every operation (create and update)
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL, clinician_headers, create_observation

def test_diagnostic_report_status_transition_registered_to_final():
    obs_id = create_observation()
    ref = [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json={"resourceType": "DiagnosticReport", "status": "registered",
              "code": {"text": "Transition test"}, "result": ref},
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    report = create_resp.json()
    assert report["status"] == "registered"
    rid = report["id"]
    report["status"] = "final"
    update_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport/{rid}",
        json=report,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert update_resp.status_code == 200
    assert update_resp.json()["status"] == "final"
    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"DiagnosticReport/{rid}"},
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert audit.json()["total"] >= 2

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_accept_reject_decision_by_result_presence
actions: Case A: POST with result field absent entirely – expect 422
Case B: POST with result field present but empty list – expect 422
Case C: POST with result containing one valid reference – expect 201
trace: R-HLT-087: decision table covering absent, empty, and populated result combinations
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL, clinician_headers, create_observation

def test_diagnostic_report_accept_reject_decision_by_result_presence():
    hdrs = clinician_headers()
    base = {"resourceType": "DiagnosticReport", "status": "final",
            "code": {"text": "DT test"}}
    # Case A: result key absent
    resp_a = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
                           json=base, headers=hdrs)
    assert resp_a.status_code == 422, f"Case A: {resp_a.status_code}"
    # Case B: result key present, empty list
    payload_b = {**base, "result": []}
    resp_b = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
                           json=payload_b, headers=hdrs)
    assert resp_b.status_code == 422, f"Case B: {resp_b.status_code}"
    # Case C: result key present with one reference
    obs_id = create_observation()
    payload_c = {**base, "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]}
    resp_c = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
                           json=payload_c, headers=hdrs)
    assert resp_c.status_code == 201, f"Case C: {resp_c.status_code}"
